Martin County FHA Loans

FHA loans in Martin County, Florida

FHA loans across Martin County with as little as 3.5% down. Here's the county's 2026 FHA loan limit, where FHA fits on this Treasure Coast county, and how to get pre-approved.

Martin County Home Loans

FHA loans in Martin County, Florida

Martin County, anchored by the charming city of Stuart, is one of the Treasure Coast's most desirable areas, known for its waterways, preserved small-town character, and strict growth management. That desirability keeps coastal prices high, but inland communities like Palm City and parts of Stuart offer homes within reach. The FHA loan limit covers much of the county's mid-market inventory.

Martin County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Martin County

With the 2026 FHA floor of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Martin County home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). That covers much of Martin County's mid-market and inland homes, though waterfront and luxury coastal properties often exceed it. A handful of Florida counties are set higher, so we confirm Martin County's exact figure with HUD.

Coastal desirability and inland value

Martin County's waterfront commands premium prices that can exceed FHA limits, but inland and mid-market homes in Palm City, Stuart, and Hobe Sound are workable with FHA — good for first-time buyers. For condos (common near the water), confirm FHA approval via our condo guide. We'll help you target the Martin County homes that fit your FHA budget.

Communities We Serve

FHA loans across Martin County

We help FHA buyers throughout Martin County, including Stuart, Palm City, Jensen Beach, and Hobe Sound. Wherever you are in the county, we will explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment, and get you pre-approved fast. Start with our first-time buyer guide or estimate your payment with the FHA calculator.

Down payment help in Martin County

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Martin County buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Martin County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Martin County for 2026?+
Martin County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers much of the county's mid-market inventory. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Where does FHA work best in Martin County?+
In inland and mid-market communities like Palm City, Stuart, and Hobe Sound; waterfront and luxury coastal homes often exceed FHA limits.
Can I buy a Martin County condo with FHA?+
Yes, if the building is FHA-approved or the unit qualifies for Single-Unit Approval and you'll live there. We check first.
How much down payment do I need in Martin County?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Martin County?+
Yes — Stuart, Palm City, Jensen Beach, Hobe Sound, and every community in the county.
